tick_queue_pending.name: semver-range
tick_queue_pending.idea: Semver range calculator — paste a range expression and a list of versions, instantly see which match with pass/fail colors and per-comparison explanation
tick_queue_pending.rationale: Devs constantly need to know whether a package version satisfies a range (^, ~, >=, ||) when debugging package.json dependency conflicts. No offline tool does this cleanly. Implements the full npm semver spec in client-side JS: tilde, caret, comparators, wildcards, union ranges.

tick_queue_pending.name: unix-ts
tick_queue_pending.idea: Unix timestamp debugger — paste any timestamp (auto-detects seconds vs ms), see ISO 8601, UTC, local, RFC 2822, SQL DATETIME, relative time, all with copy buttons; reverse lookup: pick a date and get the epoch
tick_queue_pending.rationale: Backend devs and ops engineers read raw timestamps from logs multiple times a day. No offline tool auto-detects ms vs s, shows all formats simultaneously, and works as a pinned-tab PWA with zero deps. Tight scope, zero dependencies, daily-use utility.

tick_queue_pending.name: uuid-inspector
tick_queue_pending.idea: UUID inspector — paste any UUID, get version detection (v1/v3/v4/v5/v7), decoded timestamp (v1 Gregorian 60-bit + v7 Unix ms), MAC extraction (v1), namespace detection (v3/v5), format validator, bulk mode (one per line), and a generator for all versions
tick_queue_pending.rationale: Backend devs and DBAs encounter UUIDs in every log, DB, and API response. v7 (time-ordered) is now default in Rails 7.1, Laravel 11, and many ORMs so devs urgently need to decode its embedded timestamp. No good offline tool does version-decode + timestamp extraction. Zero external deps, pure JS math, tight scope, ships in one session. Gemini MCP unavailable this session — viability assessed internally.
